Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative existential clause with relative verb construction
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Every family or community has its own problems.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: O mũciĩ ũrĩ na matatizo mawo.
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, family discussions, community mediation
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional Kikuyu proverb emphasizing communal realism about hardship.
First Example Sentence: Gũtirĩ mũciĩ ũtakĩa thĩna, nĩguo tũrĩ hamwe gũcokeria ũrĩa ũrĩ mwega.
Second Example Sentence: Wĩ mũnene wa kũigua atĩ gũtirĩ mũciĩ ũtakĩa thĩna.
Third Example Sentence: Nĩ maũndũ ma muoyo, gũtirĩ mũciĩ ũtakĩa thĩna.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Infinitive verb phrase with instrumental complement
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To cause conflict through gossip or reckless speech.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in oral culture where speech shapes social harmony.
First Example Sentence: Ndũkũhũthĩre mũciĩ na mĩromo ya ũhoro ũtarĩ wa ma.
Second Example Sentence: Aahũthĩrire mũciĩ na mĩromo nginya andũ makĩũrana.
Third Example Sentence: Tigana na kũhũthĩra mũciĩ na mĩromo itarĩ ya wendo.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To grow strong and resilient over time.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Refers to the sacred fig tree (mũgumo), symbol of endurance in Kikuyu spirituality.
First Example Sentence: Mwana ũcio akũraga ta mũtĩ wa mũgumo rĩrĩa arĩ na ithomo.
Second Example Sentence: Twĩhoke gũkũra ta mũtĩ wa mũgumo twĩ hamwe.
Third Example Sentence: Gũkũra ta mũtĩ wa mũgumo nĩ kũmenya kwĩyũrũra.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Infinitive clause with embedded relative clause
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To feel collective responsibility or shared pain.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Kũigua thĩna hamwe na andũ angĩ.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Draws from Kikuyu communal philosophy of shared identity (ũhĩrĩga).
First Example Sentence: Mũthamaki mwĩrũ akĩigua ũrĩa mũhĩrĩga ũgũigua.
Second Example Sentence: Nĩ wega kũigua ũrĩa mũhĩrĩga ũgũigua mbere ya kũamua.
Third Example Sentence: Tũrĩ hamwe rĩu tũkĩigua ũrĩa mũhĩrĩga ũgũigua.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Ngigi (locust) imagery reflects stubborn inattentiveness in agrarian folklore.
First Example Sentence: Ndũkũrĩe matũ ma ngigi rĩrĩa wĩheaga ũhoro.
Second Example Sentence: Aarĩ mũndũ wa kũrĩa matũ ma ngigi na ndeto cia atumia.
Third Example Sentence: Tigana na kũrĩa matũ ma ngigi,igua ũhoro mwega.

Idiom (Kikuyu): Gũtonga na mũtĩ ũrĩa ũtarĩ mĩgũnda
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Agricultural metaphor reflecting Kikuyu farming heritage.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Kikuyu): ɣũtóŋà nà mũtî ũɾîà ũtáɾî míɣṹnda
First Example Sentence: Ndũgũtonga na mũtĩ ũrĩa ũtarĩ mĩgũnda.
Second Example Sentence: Andũ angĩ matongaga na mũtĩ ũrĩa ũtarĩ mĩgũnda.
Third Example Sentence: Tũtige gũtonga na mũtĩ ũrĩa ũtarĩ mĩgũnda.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: From traditional clan-based dispute resolution rituals.
First Example Sentence: Twĩ hamwe kũheana mĩhĩrĩga thutha wa gũcookania.
Second Example Sentence: Nĩ wega kũheana mĩhĩrĩga mbere ya gũtukũ.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects social belonging as central in Kikuyu worldview.
First Example Sentence: Ndĩkũigua ta ndũrĩ mũndũ rĩrĩa ndĩ hamwe na nyũmba ĩno.
Second Example Sentence: Mwana ũcio akĩigua ta ndũrĩ mũndũ thĩinĩ wa kirasi.
Third Example Sentence: Ndũkũigue ta ndũrĩ mũndũ, wĩ wa mũhĩrĩga.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: River (rũĩ) imagery symbolizes cycles and return in Kikuyu cosmology.
First Example Sentence: Nĩgũcokia rũĩ mũciĩ rĩrĩa ũheaga ũhoro mũũru.
Second Example Sentence: Aarĩ na ũhoti wa gũcokia rũĩ mũciĩ.
Third Example Sentence: Tigana na gũcokia rũĩ mũciĩ, heana thayũ.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Heart (ngoro) central in Kikuyu metaphors of emotion.
First Example Sentence: Ndũkũmene ngoro ya mũndũ ũcio na ndeto ciaku.
Second Example Sentence: Ũhoro ũcio wamenire ngoro yakwa mũno.
Third Example Sentence: Kũmena ngoro nĩ ũndũ ũtarĩ wa wendo.
